title: Drum Rack Setup
summary: Build a Drum Rack from scratch to load individual samples onto pads, control velocity layers, and apply per-pad effects chains.
steps: Drag 'Drum Rack' from Instruments > Drum Rack in the browser into a new MIDI track
Click a pad to select it, then drag a sample (wav/aiff) from the browser onto that pad — or drag directly onto a pad
Double-click the pad to open the Simpler inside and adjust sample start point, loop, and pitch
Repeat for each drum sound: kick on C1, snare/clap on D1, closed hat on F#1, open hat on G#1 (standard GM layout)
Click the 'Show/Hide Chain List' button (vertical bars icon) to see each pad as a chain — you can add FX per pad here
In the 'Pad View' (bottom left), set Velocity > Volume to respond to note velocity, or fix volume by removing the velocity mod
tips: Right-click a pad > 'Slice to New MIDI Track' to chop a loop and map each slice to its own pad automatically
Use 'Choke Groups' (the number in the pad view) so open hat chokes closed hat — just like a real hi-hat
Stack two samples on the same pad using 'Add Chain' in the chain list for layered sounds (e.g. sub + click for kick)
Hold Ctrl/Cmd when dragging a sample to a pad to replace the existing sample instead of stacking

images.caption: Click a pad to select it — a row of controls appears below the 16-pad grid. Set Choke to '1' on both the open hat and closed hat pads so the closed hat cuts the open hat when it fires. The pad grid shows C1 bottom-left: load kick on C1, clap on D1, closed hat on F#1, open hat on G#1.

ableton_version_note: Drum Rack available since Ableton Live 7. Slicing requires Live Standard or Suite.
